other_observers: Nick Komar , Sandy Winkler , Judy Sanborn.; Jointly found with Nick Komar ,
specific_location: South side of Elevenmile Reservoir SP , access road to Roger's Mountain fishing access point .
description: In comparison with adjacent Horned Larks , size was a little shorter and compact , short tail , fairly long primary wing projection . Overall coloration was a pale sandy brown , pale supercilium , pale brownish auricular patch , underparts and flanks off white and unstreaked with ( from photo ) faint blackish band across upper breast. Three chestnut median coverts also visible . In flight showed an upside down black T on a white tail. Long hind claw also noted.Behaviors: Feeding on the ground alongside the dirt access road to the reservoir in company with Horned Larks . Made a couple of short bouncy flights before landing and commencing feeding on small seeds on the road.Call: dry rattle callPlumage: winter
photographer: Photographer: John Drummond, John Drummond, John Drummond
